title: A4 Response store: Neon jsonb repositories (responses, in-progress state) + DO Spaces storage adapter decision
description: Re-scoped per owner decision 2026-07-14 (see bd memory owner-decision-2026-07-14-firebase-firestore-likely): Firebase/Firestore dropped. Questionnaire responses + in-progress state are stored in Neon Postgres jsonb columns/tables instead of Firestore. This issue covers: (1) response-store repositories over the jsonb tables (align with A2 schema), (2) resolving the object-storage question for branding assets + legacy PDFs — DO Spaces is the natural candidate — and implementing the storage adapter interface accordingly. Spec ref: docs/spec/04-data-model.md (Firestore sections are superseded by this re-scope). Affects C2 (progress save/resume) and K2 (legacy PDF import).

title: Epic A: Foundations & platform shell
description: Monorepo, DB schema, auth, queues, design system, hosting, audit. Spec: development-files/assessify-spec/03,04,05,15. Read 00-README.md first.

title: M4 Org admin UI: org CRUD (platform), pricing editor, client product-access editor
description: Admin surface: (a) super_admin org management (create org, assign products to orgs); (b) org-admin pages — price list editor per product (language x currency rows, integer minor units), client product-access editor (default vs per-client grants for custom products); (c) minimal context indicator in the shell (full switcher can follow with J2 owner dashboard). Follows existing (admin) conventions from B1/B3/D2.

title: M3 Service invariants: org-bound ordering, product access checks, price resolution
description: Order service + wizard: (a) invariant — order.client.organization_id == order.product.organization_id (typed error otherwise); (b) product picker/listOrderable filters to the caller's context org AND client access (default_access OR client_product_access grant); (c) unit price resolves from product_prices by product+language+currency (fallback to products.retail_price until orgs set prices); (d) client directory scoped per org; (e) invitation/tenant paths unchanged (branding stays per product). Admin UI touch-ups in the order wizard + client pages to reflect org scoping.

title: M2 Authz re-scope: org-scoped assessment_admin + CallerContext org scope + context list
description: Re-scope authz for the org model: assessment_admin role rows scope by organization_id (was product_id); CallerContext helpers (packages/domain caller-context, productScopeIds and friends) gain orgScopeIds and product-via-org resolution; permission matrix updates — org admins may: set product pricing (product_prices), manage client_product_access, view all their org's clients/orders/dashboards; platform (super_admin) creates products and assigns orgs. Services updated to the new checks (products, questionnaires read paths, orders list scoping, error queue stays super_admin). Also expose a listContexts(caller) capability (platform / orgs / clients from role_assignments) as the data source for the future context switcher UI — UI itself is M4/J2 territory.

title: M1 Schema: organizations table + org FKs + product price list + client product access
description: Migration (packages/db): (a) organizations table (id uuid, name, slug, status, settlement fields; move products.connected_stripe_account_id here — royalty rates revenue_split_pct/royalty_policy STAY on products); (b) products.organization_id FK not null; (c) clients.organization_id FK not null (same real company under 2 orgs = 2 client rows; keep global client_number sequence); (d) products.default_access boolean (org-wide default) + client_product_access grant table (client_id, product_id, unique) for restricted/custom products; (e) product_prices price list: product_id, language, currency, unit_price bigint minor units, unique(product_id, language, currency) — org-managed pricing per language edition (do NOT overload questionnaire_versions.variant, that axis is rater variants); (f) role_assignments.organization_id FK (for org-scoped assessment_admin); (g) house/direct-sales client: repurpose is_platform_retail toward one house client per org (rename or document). Domain package: Organization entity + Zod schemas, updated Product/Client schemas. Seed thinking: no production data exists; local volumes just get reset.
notes: Addendum 2026-07-21b: also DROP clients.is_platform_retail (owner decision: no special direct-sales client concept — a direct-sales site is an ordinary client + partner API key). Remove code references (client directory listPlaceable filter, D2 wizard picker exclusion). Migration may assume products/clients tables are empty (no production environment exists yet).

title: Epic M: Organizations & org-scoped tenancy
description: Owner decisions 2026-07-21 (see bd memory owner-decisions-2026-07-21-arnold-organization-model): introduce the Organization entity between Platform and Product. Platform -> Org (product owner company) -> Client -> Taker. Products and clients belong to orgs; Stripe Connect + royalty settlement at org level; org-set pricing per product+language; per-client product access grants; context switcher over role scopes. Blocks correct implementation of H (billing), J (royalties/Connect), G1 (retail house client).

title: Redo A7 for Coolify + Hetzner: rewrite/replace .do/app.yaml, docker-compose or Coolify config, deploy docs
description:
Owner decision 2026-07-20: hosting moves from DigitalOcean App Platform to Coolify (self-hosted PaaS triggered from the GitHub repo, no DO account needed), and object storage moves to Hetzner Object Storage (S3-compatible) instead of DO Spaces. This supersedes most of A7's original deliverable.

Needed:
- Replace .do/app.yaml + .do/app.staging.yaml (currently marked SUPERSEDED, kept for reference only) with whatever Coolify actually needs: likely a docker-compose.yml per app (web, worker, pdf-service) plus Coolify's own env/service config in its UI, OR a Dockerfile per app if Coolify builds from those directly. apps/pdf-service already has a Dockerfile (docker-compose is probably needed for web + worker).
- Valkey/Redis (BullMQ backing store) becomes a self-hosted Coolify service (or managed if Coolify offers one) instead of DO Managed Valkey.
- Rewrite docs/deploy.md: drop the doctl/DO provisioning checklist, replace with Coolify app setup (connect GitHub repo, build packs, env var entry, custom domains + TLS) and Hetzner Object Storage bucket creation.
- Object storage adapter is already generic (packages/adapters/src/storage/providers/s3.ts, S3_* env vars) — no code change needed there, just the deploy docs/config.
- Neon Postgres presumably stays (not DO-specific) — confirm with owner.

Not urgent: current .env.example already reflects the target env var shape (S3_*, Coolify-neutral wording) so local dev / a first Coolify app can be configured today without this issue.

title: Wire scoring dispatch into web composition root (submit -> scoring)
description: E1 left the web submit path on the no-op ScoringDispatcher because apps/web was outside its footprint. Wire getQuestionnaireSessionService({ scoring: getScoringService({ queue }) }) in the web composition root using the BullMQ queue from apps/web/lib/queue.ts (D5 added it; needs VALKEY_URL). Until then, respondent submits do not enqueue scoring.
close_reason: Wired: apps/web/lib/questionnaire-session.ts composition root passes getScoringService({queue}) into getQuestionnaireSessionService when a queue URL is configured; actions.ts + page.tsx use it. Submits now dispatch scoring in any env with REDIS_URL set (incl. docker compose).

E5 becomes: build the actual PRO-D template file(s) + the upload/versioning path (report_template_versions table exists) + golden-file render test through pdf-service. Email templates for products follow the same uploaded-file approach (D4's templateIds map already treats templates as external references).

title: E3 Report assembly + release controls
description: Spec: development-files/assessify-spec/09: render-data snapshot to reports.data; auto-release policy; hold/release per respondent or bulk.
notes: Re-scope 2026-07-21 (Arnold): report templates are manually built pixel-perfect HTML files per product, UPLOADED to S3 storage (storage adapter) — not code, not generated from branding tokens. Report assembly = fetch template from storage + merge score/respondent data + serve web view; pdf-service already consumes raw HTML+CSS so the same file drives print. Some products are web-only (no PDF template) — template record should mark web/pdf availability; release controls unaffected.

title: E2 Scoring adapter: Pro-Logic integration (POST /v2/score sync + 'scored' HMAC webhook)
description: Full contract now known: docs/pro-logic-openapi.json (OpenAPI 3.1 v2.0), bearer auth, POST /v2/score (one-shot registration+tools+scoring, SYNCHRONOUS) is likely the primary integration path — simpler than the 3-call register/submit-tool/score sequence. Response = ResultEnvelope (assessment_id, external_id, scored_at, language, format, norms{set_id, provisional}, scopes{...}). Webhook 'scored': HMAC-SHA256 of raw body in X-Signature (key's webhook secret), event name in X-Event, POST to the key's webhook_url, retried with backoff on non-2xx. See bd memory pro-logic-scoring-api-full-contract-2026-07 for the complete endpoint/field list (tools enum, scopes enum, idempotency-key semantics, per-item 422 validation shape). Build decision needed: since Pro-Logic scoring is synchronous per-call, this may not need E1's originally-scoped async/callback/watchdog machinery at all — could be a synchronous external adapter (like E1's sync_internal shape, just calling out over HTTP) with the webhook used only as a secondary confirmation channel. Re-scope after reading the memory in full.
notes: CRITICAL 2026-07-21c: external_id sent to Pro-Logic MUST be respondents.id (stable across a person's multiple orders/rescores via D2's find-or-create-by-email), never order_id/session_id. Reason: Pro-Logic charges Assessify a royalty per external_id for royalty-bearing products; reusing the same external_id on a rescore of the same person avoids a duplicate royalty charge. Getting this wrong is a real billing bug. See bd memory for full reasoning.

description: Spec: development-files/assessify-spec/04,07,11.

title: Decide: offline payment confirmation actor — admin-only or allow client_admin?
description: Spec 06's transition table gives payment_succeeded to system|super_admin, but client admins can place orders; a client_admin-initiated offline payment fails the actor check in orderService.transition. Owner decision needed: either offline orders are confirmed by admins only (wizard should say so), or the transition table adds client_admin for the offline path. D3 passes the real caller through (correct per current table). Affects D2 wizard UX.
close_reason: Decided 2026-07-20 (Arnold): admin-only, no code change needed. The platform's commercial relationship is with the client (who pays Assessify/the product owner for the order) — the respondent/end-taker pays the client off-platform separately. Since the client_admin IS the payer on an offline/invoice order, they cannot be the one confirming their own payment was received (separation of duties). Current code already enforces this: payment_succeeded actors are ['system','super_admin'] only (packages/domain/src/orders/state-machine.ts:105). D2's order wizard should surface this as expected behavior (client places the order and pays by invoice/bank transfer off-platform; a staff admin marks it paid once reconciled), not as a bug.

title: Payments hardening: unique index on payments(provider, provider_ref) + optional event-id dedupe table
description: D3's webhook idempotency follows spec 06 (upsert by provider_ref, replay-safe CAS) but the DB doesn't enforce uniqueness on (provider, provider_ref), and a by-event-id dedupe table (payment_webhook_events, unique event_id) would harden replay handling further. Needs a packages/db migration. Neither blocks correctness under Stripe redelivery semantics.

title: C2xB4 integration: resolve translation strings server-side in renderer loadState
description: C2's renderer humanizes translation keys as a fallback (labelFromKey in apps/web/app/(respondent)/a/[token]/q/_lib/renderer.ts). B4's translationService.resolve(productId, language, keys) is now merged. Wire loadState (packages/services/src/questionnaire-session/) to resolve the version's keys (collectTranslationKeys from the translations service) for the session language and return resolved strings in RendererState; drop labelFromKey. Prerequisite for C6 language switcher.

title: Questionnaire import: translation-key presence check + warnings channel
description: Spec 07: import should verify every *Key in a questionnaire definition exists in translation_strings for the product's default language (error) and warn for other languages; also warn when an active version's keys disappear in the next version. B3's import returns success/error only — needs a warnings channel in the service result and a translation-strings repository (lands with B4, asy-ow0.4). Files: packages/services/src/questionnaires/.

title: Unit-of-work: make state change + audit write one DB transaction
description: B1, B3 and D1 services record audit events in a separate write from the entity mutation (documented in code comments). Introduce a unit-of-work abstraction so order/product/questionnaire state changes and their audit_log rows commit atomically. Touches packages/repositories (transaction delegation) + packages/services (product, questionnaire-version, order services).

title: Valkey-backed PinAttemptStore for respondent PIN lockout
description: C1 shipped PIN attempt counters behind the PinAttemptStore port (packages/repositories/src/respondent-access/pin-attempt-store.ts) with an in-memory default — correct per process only. Spec 05 says counters live in Valkey so lockout holds across web replicas. Implement a Valkey-backed store using the queue infra's connection config and wire it in the default composition root.

title: Refresh progress artifact (run on a low-level model: haiku/sonnet)
description:
Recurring housekeeping — refresh the published progress artifact after each wave/section merges. RUN THIS ON A CHEAP MODEL (spawn the subagent with model=haiku or sonnet; it does NOT need Opus). Artifact URL: https://claude.ai/code/artifact/260d98ca-3d1c-4912-ad36-f9c9b7c8e089

Procedure (self-contained):
1. In the assessify-platform repo run: bd stats ; bd list --status=closed -n 0 ; and get the test count: pnpm test 2>&1 | grep -oE '[0-9]+ passed' | grep -oE '[0-9]+' | awk '{s+=$1} END {print s}'
2. WebFetch the artifact URL above to retrieve its CURRENT HTML (the original source scratchpad file is session-specific and gone, so fetch the live version).
3. Update, keeping the existing Ember design / embedded font / layout UNCHANGED — only refresh data: masthead stat-strip numbers (issues shipped, tests passing); each epic card's ratio + status pill + per-issue dots; and the 'recently decided / open question' cards. Cross-check epic completion against bd (Epics A-partial, B, C, D, M complete; E mostly done; F/G/H/I/J/K/L per bd).
4. Write the updated HTML to a file, then call the Artifact tool with that file_path AND url='https://claude.ai/code/artifact/260d98ca-3d1c-4912-ad36-f9c9b7c8e089' so it updates IN PLACE (do not mint a new URL). Keep the same favicon/title.

This is a leave-open recurring task — do NOT close it; just re-run it after waves. Reopen/refresh rather than duplicate.

title: report_template_versions: promote storageKey/capabilities from config jsonb to real columns
description: E3 stored uploaded-template metadata as {storageKey, capabilities} inside report_template_versions.config with sentinel component_key='uploaded_html' because the columns predate the uploaded-HTML re-scope and packages/db was outside its footprint. Migration: add storage_key text + capabilities columns (or rename component_key), migrate the service/repo mapping. Also wire clients.notification_overrides.reportRelease (client-level release-policy override, documented but unwired) once the client repo exposes overrides.

title: ProductRepository: org-filtered list query (replace bounded-scan listOrgProducts)
description: M4's organizationService.listOrgProducts and listOrderable both filter a bounded 500-row product scan in the service layer. Add an organization_id filter to ProductRepository.list (packages/repositories/src/products/) and migrate both callers. Also consider product/client count projections for the org list page. Cheap, non-urgent until catalogues grow.

title: Session language: respondent_sessions.language goes stale after in-questionnaire switch
description: C6 decision: single source of truth for the respondent's language is questionnaire_responses.language (seeded from the session row at response creation, updated by setLanguage). Spec 07 says switching changes respondent_sessions.language — that column now goes stale after a switch. If notifications (D6 reminders), reports (E3/E5) or exports read session.language they'll use the pre-switch value. Either add a session-repo language setter called from setLanguage, or make those consumers read the response row. Small, decide when E3 lands.

title: docs/spec/06: order transition table missing 'sent -> email_error' row
description: D5 (invitation dispatch, merged) added the transition sent --invitation_failed--> email_error to the domain state machine (packages/domain/src/orders/state-machine.ts), because a hard email bounce always arrives AFTER the order has already moved to 'sent' (invitations dispatched) -- but docs/spec/06-orders-and-state-machine.md's transition table only documents approved -> email_error. The code is correct and shipped; this issue is just to bring the spec doc back in sync with it so the doc stays the source of truth. Small, low-risk doc edit -- one table row + a line of prose.

title: Renderer: randomizeSections support with stable per-session shuffle seed
description: Spec 07 settings.randomizeSections is not honoured — C2 renders sections in document order (deferred: needs a stable per-session seed so resume keeps the same order). Derive seed from respondent session id, shuffle in the questionnaire-session service (not the UI), respect visibility evaluator.

title: Spec docs: re-scope Firestore/Firebase references to Neon jsonb + DO Spaces
description: Owner decision 2026-07-14 dropped Firebase. docs/spec/03-architecture.md, 04-data-model.md (Firestore collections + GDPR erasure section), 09/14 (legacy PDF storage) still describe Firestore/Firebase Storage. Update to: responses in questionnaire_responses jsonb (packages/db/src/schema/responses.ts), objects in DO Spaces. Also decide GDPR erasure treatment of free_text answer values in jsonb, and update reports.legacy_pdf_path comment (Firebase path -> Spaces key). Keep a supersession note rather than rewriting history.
notes: Also: confirm Pro-Logic webhook signature ENCODING with Arntek (adapter accepts hex and base64, both constant-time) and the /v2/reference/scopes response shape — both undocumented in their OpenAPI. And: buildScoringAnswers strips free_text before dispatch, so a reflections scope fed by free-text tools can't work without a per-config PII exception (flagged in E2).

title: L3 Self-serve custom domain onboarding

value: Workflow rules from Arnold (updated 2026-07-22): (1) Multi-agent waves: STOP after each wave (merge/close/push, report) and wait for go-ahead — parallel waves kept hitting Claude session usage limits. (2) From wave 7 on, tighter still while credits are low: run wave sections SEQUENTIALLY, ONE agent at a time; after each section merges, stop and wait — Arnold manually checks usage credits before authorizing the next section. Wave 7 sections: 7a E6 notifications, 7b E5 PRO-D template, 7c H1 entitlements, 7d F2 (needs Coolify re-scope first, see asy-2m9).

Owner decision 2026-07-14 (updated 2026-07-20): Firebase/Firestore dropped. Responses + in-progress state are Neon Postgres jsonb (packages/db/src/schema/responses.ts, built in A4). Object storage is Hetzner Object Storage / S3-compatible (packages/adapters/src/storage/providers/s3.ts, S3_* env vars) — DO Spaces was an interim guess, now superseded. Affects A4 (done), C2 (done), K2 (legacy PDF import, not yet built).

Owner decisions 2026-07-21 (Arnold) — ORGANIZATION MODEL, supersedes the flat product-owner reading of spec 01/05. Hierarchy is Platform -> Organization (product owner company) -> Client -> Assessment taker. (1) New first-class 'organizations' entity: products belong to an org (PRO-D Premium/Advance/Core = 3 products, 1 org); Stripe Connect onboarding + royalty SETTLEMENT identity live at org level, royalty RATES stay per product. (2) Clients belong to an organization (clients.organization_id). Same real-world company as client of 2 orgs = 2 client rows; the context switcher surfaces both. Within a context, clients can only see/order that org's products. (3) Product access within an org: products are either org-default (available to all the org's clients) or restricted (custom products) with explicit per-client access grants. (4) Pricing: PLATFORM creates products and assigns them to orgs; ORGS set pricing, and pricing can differ per sellable variant — Arnold's example is language editions (PRO-D Premium English vs Spanish priced differently). NOTE vocabulary collision: questionnaire_versions.variant already means rater variant (self/manager); model org pricing as a per-product price list keyed by language (+ currency), not by overloading 'variant'. (5) Interface stays ONE admin surface with permission gating + a context switcher (contexts = role_assignments grouped by scope: platform / each org / each client; single context = default, no switcher shown). (6) assessment_admin re-scopes to organization_id. (7) Direct sales: house client per org (assumed, not explicitly confirmed) so retail/direct orders reuse the whole order machinery. (8) Templates (2026-07-21): report + email templates are manually built per product as pixel-perfect HTML files, uploaded to S3 storage — NOT code, NOT heavy branding customization; some products are web-only with no PDF template; affects E3/E5 scope.

Pro-Logic API usage details (2026-07-21b, Arnold): (1) Auth is per-call bearer API key. (2) Each PRODUCT has its own access_code (format ac_xxxx) — this is the 'access_code' param on /v2/assessments/{id}/score and /v2/score (scores requested must be within the code's allowance, 403 otherwise). So products.scoring_config (or a new column) needs to store this access_code alongside which API key to use. (3) Tools ARE submitted separately/incrementally as the client completes them — PUT /v2/assessments/{id}/tools/{tool} per tool, scoring (POST .../score) happens later once enough tools are done. This means E2 is NOT just the one-shot POST /v2/score — for Assessify's real flow (respondent completes questionnaire sections over time, C2's autosave), tool submission should likely happen per-section/tool as answers land (or in one batch at questionnaire submit), with the score call triggered separately after. Revise the 'maybe simpler, one-shot' note from the earlier memory — the incremental per-tool submit is probably the actual integration shape, with /v2/score as a shortcut only useful if all tool data is captured in one place before scoring. (4) Not every product uses every tool — GET /v2/reference/scopes returns the scope catalog including REQUIRED TOOLS per scope, norm behavior, and output shape — E2 must call this (or cache it) to know which tools a given product's scopes actually need, so only relevant questionnaire sections map to tools for that product. See also GET /v2/reference/questions and /v2/reference/translations for tool question definitions and copy. Combine with pro-logic-scoring-api-full-contract-2026-07 memory for the full endpoint list.

Owner decision 2026-07-21b (Arnold): NO special 'house client' / retail-umbrella concept — direct sales get no special treatment. A future direct-sales website is just an API consumer: partner API key (Epic I) tied to an ordinary client under the org, orders flow through the normal machinery. Retire clients.is_platform_retail (drop in M1 migration; remove code references e.g. D2 wizard picker exclusion). Reframes G1: public product page is an API-key-backed storefront, not a special order path.

Pro-Logic external_id semantics + royalty billing (2026-07-21c, Arnold — IMPORTANT, affects billing correctness): external_id must be respondents.id (the stable, find-or-create-by-email respondent row — packages/db/src/schema/parties.ts), NOT order_id or respondent_session_id. Reasoning: Pro-Logic bills Assessify a ROYALTY per external_id (per distinct scored person) for products that have a royalty in their system. If the same person (respondent) is rescored later — e.g. they retake/update answers under a new order, or revisit via a retail account (assessment_taker, respondents.user_id) — reusing the SAME external_id = respondents.id lets Pro-Logic recognize it as an update to an existing scored person and skip charging a new royalty. Using order_id or session_id as external_id would fabricate a new royalty charge on every rescore of the same human, which is a real money bug, not just a data-modeling nicety. CONSEQUENCE FOR E2/E1 BUILD: the scoring adapter's outbound registration payload (POST /v2/assessments or /v2/score, field 'registration.external_id' / top-level 'external_id') must resolve and pass respondents.id, not any order/session-scoped id. Also relevant to E2's own royalty tracking (H2/J1) — Assessify's OWN royalty ledger to the product owner should probably key off the same respondent identity for consistency, though that's a separate ledger. See also pro-logic-scoring-api-full-contract-2026-07 and the incremental-tools memory for the rest of the contract.

Pro-Logic scoring API — FULL CONTRACT (2026-07-21, from docs/pro-logic-openapi.json, OpenAPI 3.1 v2.0, checked into the repo). Auth: bearer token (webhook_url + webhook secret configured per API key out of band). Flow for Assessify/E2: POST /v2/assessments (register: firstname/middlename/lastname/email/language[en,fr,pt]/gender[M,F,null]/dob[unused]/external_id[echoed]) -> id; PUT /v2/assessments/{id}/tools/{tool} per tool (tool enum: reflections, personalmotivators, areamissions, abilitiesfilter, personalstyle, personalexpectations, person, role, organization; body {responses: {q:a} map OR [{q,a}] list, q 1-based}, 422 gives structured {tool,q,rule,expected,got}); POST /v2/assessments/{id}/score (SYNCHRONOUS scoring; body {scopes[] enum mcs.m/mcs.c/mcs.s/mcs/pro.person/pro.role/pro.org/insights/reflections/full default [full], format keys|strings default keys, language, norms male|female|pooled|<id> defaults from gender, access_code (falls back to key default, must cover requested scopes or 403), audit boolean -> trace at GET /v2/assessments/{id}/results/audit}) -> ResultEnvelope. ALSO: POST /v2/score is a one-shot registration+tools+scoring in one call (needs registration+tools body) - likely the simpler integration path for E2 rather than 3 separate calls. All mutating endpoints accept Idempotency-Key header (same key+payload replays, same key+different payload = 409). GET /v2/assessments/{id}, /results, /results/audit, plus GET /v2/reference/{languages,questions,translations,scopes} for definitions/copy. ResultEnvelope: {assessment_id, external_id, scored_at, language, format, norms:{set_id, provisional}, scopes:{...one entry per requested scope}} - record norms.set_id forever per spec's own note. WEBHOOK 'scored': POST to the key's webhook_url after each successful score call, HMAC-SHA256 of RAW body in X-Signature header (key's webhook secret), event name in X-Event header (const 'scored'), body = ResultEnvelope + {event:'scored'}; Pro-Logic retries with backoff on non-2xx, any 2xx acks. This confirms 'synchronous scoring with optional HMAC webhook' from the docs page: E2's real design is likely POST /v2/score (or assessments+tools+score) synchronously per session, store result immediately, with the webhook as a secondary/redundant delivery path (or used only if Assessify ever calls score async) - decide at build time whether E2 even needs the async/watchdog machinery originally scoped, or if this collapses into E1's sync_internal-style adapter calling an external HTTP API synchronously. Full JSON at docs/pro-logic-openapi.json.
